concepts.title: Pascal's Law
concepts.description: Pascal's Law states that the pressure applied to a confined fluid is transmitted undiminished in every direction and acts upon every point of the confining vessel or surrounding fluid. This is crucial for understanding hydraulic systems and pressure transmission.

concepts.title: Viscosity Definition
concepts.description: According to NCERT, viscosity is the measure of the internal friction of a fluid, which is the friction between the different layers of the fluid as they slide past one another. It's essential to remember that viscosity is a measure of a fluid's resistance to flow.

concepts.title: Bernoulli's Principle
concepts.description: Bernoulli's Principle is frequently asked in exams, which states that an increase in the speed of a fluid occurs simultaneously with a decrease in pressure or a decrease in the fluid's potential energy. This principle is crucial for understanding the behavior of fluids in motion.

concepts.title: Remembering Surface Tension
concepts.description: To remember the factors affecting surface tension, use the mnemonic 'TENSE': Temperature, External pressure, Nature of the liquid, Surface area, and External fields. This helps in recalling the key factors that influence surface tension.

concepts.title: Confusing Bulk Modulus with Compressibility
concepts.description: Students often confuse bulk modulus with compressibility. To avoid this, remember that bulk modulus (B) is the ratio of hydrostatic stress to the resulting change in volume, whereas compressibility (k) is the reciprocal of bulk modulus. The correct relation is B = 1/k.

concepts.title: Viscosity Units
concepts.description: Remember that the unit of viscosity (η) is Poiseuille (Pl) or Pascal-second (Pa·s) in SI units. In CGS units, it's measured in Poise (P). 1 Poise = 0.1 Pa·s. This is essential for solving numerical problems related to viscosity.

concepts.title: Archimedes' Principle
concepts.description: Archimedes' Principle states that the buoyant force on an object immersed in a fluid is equal to the weight of the fluid displaced by the object. This principle is crucial for understanding the behavior of objects in fluids and solving related problems.

concepts.title: Reynolds Number
concepts.description: According to NCERT, the Reynolds number (Re) is a dimensionless quantity used to predict flow patterns in different fluid flow situations. It's defined as the ratio of inertial forces to viscous forces and is given by Re = ρUL/η, where ρ is density, U is velocity, L is characteristic length, and η is viscosity.
